title: Ship the intent anchor: a durable verbatim objective, re-asserted at PreCompact, read first on compact-resume
action: Read docs/history/intent-anchor/CONTEXT.md (D1-D6) and .bee/spikes/intent-anchor/FINDINGS.md first — the spike proved the mechanism and measured it; this cell ships it for real. FOUR pieces. (A) NEW LIB `skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/intent.mjs` (mirror to .bee/bin/lib/): a small fixed-shape store at `.bee/intent/<key>.json`. Key = the active feature slug when one exists, else the session id (D2 — work that never enters a feature must still be anchorable). Fields per D1: `request` (VERBATIM — never paraphrased, this is the whole point), `acceptance`, `next_action`, plus optional `feature`/`lane`/`cell`, `do_not_reverse` (array), `stop_conditions` (array). Export: writeIntent/readIntent/advanceIntent(next_action only — request+acceptance are immutable once set)/clearIntent, and two RENDERERS: `precompactBlock(anchor)` and `resumeBlock(anchor)`. Fail-open everywhere: a missing/corrupt anchor returns null, never throws. (B) CLI: add an `intent` command group (set/show/advance/clear) in command-registry.mjs + bee.mjs dispatch, following the existing group idiom exactly. `set` requires --request and --acceptance; --next-action optional. (C) PRECOMPACT (D3): `hooks/bee-session-close.mjs` ALREADY runs on the PreCompact event (see hooks/hooks.json). Extend it so that on the compaction event it ALSO emits the anchor block, labelled so a summarizer cannot treat it as ordinary prose. It MUST stay advisory — the existing contract (concept areas/hook-runtime/advisories-and-turn-control.md B2/R14) forbids any turn-control verdict on compaction; do not touch that. Mirror to .bee/bin/hooks/. (D) COMPACT-RESUME (D4): `hooks/bee-session-init.mjs` receives payload.source and already distinguishes compact/resume from clear/startup. When the source is compact or resume AND an anchor exists, the emitted block must LEAD with the anchor (the objective) and the phase/workflow detail comes after — the ordering IS the fix, since the whole defect is that bee re-anchors on its own bookkeeping. Do NOT change handoff-adoption rules (a compacted session still never auto-adopts planned-next — that stays). D5: with no anchor, output must be byte-identical to today. (E) TESTS: anchor round-trip incl. immutability of request/acceptance under advance; PreCompact emits the anchor and stays advisory (no block verdict); a compact-source session start leads with the anchor and a no-anchor repo is byte-identical to today; and D6 — a SIMULATION over >=2 compaction boundaries showing the verbatim request survives with the anchor while a control without it loses the request. Put hook rows in hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs following its adapterRow idiom; lib/CLI tests where those live. (F) Regen ALL derived artifacts as the last step: mirror copies, render_plugin_skill_trees, release_manifest --write, onboard_bee --apply. Then the full chain BOTH ways as the very last action.

truths: the anchor stores the request VERBATIM and advance() cannot mutate request or acceptance
an anchor is writable with no active feature (D2)
PreCompact emits the anchor and remains advisory — no turn-control verdict on compaction
a compact/resume session start LEADS with the anchor, phase detail after
with no anchor present, both hooks are byte-identical to today (D5)
a >=2-compaction simulation shows the verbatim request surviving with the anchor and lost without it (D6)
node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 AND BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0
prohibitions: never emit a turn-control/block verdict on the compaction event — the advisory contract is untouched
never change handoff adoption rules (a compacted session still never auto-adopts planned-next)
never paraphrase or truncate `request` — verbatim is the mechanism
no behaviour change when no anchor exists

trace.outcome: Shipped the intent anchor: a small durable record holding the user's request VERBATIM, re-asserted at PreCompact so a summarizer cannot drop it, and read FIRST on a compact/resume start so the objective outranks bee's own phase bookkeeping. That ordering is the whole fix — the drift was a priority inversion where the scaffolding was on disk and the goal was not. Proven by a two-boundary compaction simulation with a control arm: with the anchor the verbatim request is present and leads at every boundary; without it the request is gone by boundary 2 while the scaffolding survives at full strength. The no-anchor path is strictly additive and byte-identical to before, and compaction stays advisory — the PreCompact payload carries only a systemMessage, never a turn-control verdict. Anchors are writable with no active feature, which is the case that previously had nothing holding its intent at all.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "The control arm of the D6 simulation IS the red evidence, and it reproduces the reported failure exactly. Without an anchor, after two compaction boundaries the verbatim request is GONE from the reconstituted context while bee's scaffolding is re-injected at full strength every start — the priority inversion, shown verbatim: the control output carries only '## bee v1.x / Phase: swarming / Gates: context: approved | shape: approved | execution: approved' plus a degrading '[summary of prior context]' tail, with zero bytes of the original ask. Presence is negated at boundaries 2 and 3 in the control arm, and the same rows assert the scaffolding DID survive, so the control cannot pass by being trivially empty. Measured earlier in the spike (.bee/spikes/intent-anchor): per-segment re-init 27,961 -> 1,887 tok (14.8x), and intent presence after 2 compacts absent -> present across optimistic/central/pessimistic decay curves.",
  "green_evidence_d6": "With the anchor, the verbatim request is present AND leads at boundaries 1, 2 and 3, rendered by the real shipped renderers (not a test double). Verified live by the orchestrator end-to-end on this repo: writing an anchor and then running the real session-init hook with source=compact emits '## INTENT ANCHOR — read this FIRST (the objective; bee workflow state follows below)' with the request reproduced byte-for-byte — including non-ASCII text, confirming no paraphrase or transcoding anywhere in the pipeline.",
  "green_evidence_d5": "The no-anchor path is unchanged, and the worker proved it the stronger way rather than diffing against HEAD (which goes tautological the moment this commits): the anchor path is strictly ADDITIVE. session-init asserts withAnchor.stdout.endsWith('\\n\\n' + noAnchor.stdout) — every pre-existing preamble byte identical, the anchor only a prefix; session-close asserts withMessage === anchorBlock + '\\n' + controlMessage with zero intent bytes in the control. Confirmed live by the orchestrator: this repo (no anchor) emits a compact-source preamble starting '## bee v1.11.1' exactly as before. A corrupt anchor file renders byte-identically to no anchor; clear/startup sources are byte-identical either way; Stop never emits the anchor.",
  "advisory_contract_intact": "Verified independently by the orchestrator, not taken on the worker's word: the real PreCompact invocation returns stdout whose parsed keys are exactly ['systemMessage'] — no 'decision', no 'block'. The B2/R14 contract (compaction must never carry a turn-control verdict) is untouched. Handoff adoption is also unchanged: compact + anchor + a planned-next handoff leaves the handoff on disk, the claim owner unchanged, and emits no adoption.",
  "shape": "A small fixed-shape record at .bee/intent/<key>.json (gitignored, verified at .gitignore:16): verbatim request, acceptance, one next_action, plus feature/lane/cell, do_not_reverse[] and stop_conditions[] — the set the external review asked for. Keyed active-feature -> session -> default, with retrieval walking all candidates so a CLI-written anchor is still found by a hook and a feature-written one survives that feature closing.",
  "chain": "84 suites green both with and without the agent-name prefix (83 -> 84: the new test_intent suite joined discovery), re-run independently by the orchestrator. test_intent 18 passed. test_lib_mirror, release_manifest --check, ledger_parity --check, test_skill_render all green.",
  "deviations_accepted": "Three, each argued rather than assumed. (1) writeIntent refuses to overwrite a live anchor's request/acceptance without force — the contract only required advance() not to mutate them, but a bare set that silently replaced the objective would leave 'immutable once set' unenforced at the store; re-setting the same request stays idempotent. (2) 'Active feature' is gated on the PHASE, not the feature field, because a stale slug outlives idle/compounding-complete and would attach a fresh question's anchor to a closed feature — this follows inject.mjs's existing predicate. (3) The worker staged new files with git add (no commit) because test_installers_e2e builds its staged source from git ls-files, so an untracked lib/intent.mjs was absent from the packaged tree and failed 3 plugin-first rows; staging is what makes those rows honest.",
  "orchestrator_review": "Chain re-run both ways, the D6 suite re-run, the PreCompact advisory shape checked directly, and the no-anchor and with-anchor paths exercised live on this repo. The test anchor written during verification was cleared afterwards. One flagged diagnostic dismissed: the unused `root` parameter in bee.mjs is pre-existing (same function at HEAD), merely shifted by the new import."
}
